ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and implements vertical farming systems to increase crop yields while minimizing environmental impact.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Develops and maintains vertical farming systems, including hydroponics, aeroponics, and LED lighting, to optimize crop growth and reduce water and land usage.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.Tech in Agricultural Engineering or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and technology can pursue a degree in agricultural engineering or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue certifications or bridge courses in agricultural engineering or related fields.

future_builder.description: Experienced professionals in agriculture or engineering can transition to vertical farming by acquiring advanced skills in hydroponics, aeroponics, and LED lighting.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience in agriculture or engineering

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition to vertical farming by acquiring relevant skills and building a portfolio of projects.

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Design and Implement a Vertical Farming System
